name: Veal Loin Chops with Double Tomato Sauce
recipeIngredient: 4 Veal loin chops - 1 to 1-1/2 inches thick (8 oz each)
1/4 lb Mushrooms - imported, such as oyster, chanterelles or shiitake - thinly sliced
1 can Whole peeled tomatoes - drained, juice retained (28 oz.)
1 oz Sun-dried tomatoes - reconstituted in boiling water for 1 to 2 minutes, then drained and thinly sliced
1 tbsp Basil - fresh, chopped OR 1/2 t. dried, crumbled
1/4 cup Red wine - quality dry wine only
1/2 cup Reserved tomato juice from drained tomatoes (about 1/2 to 3/4 cup)
12 oz Pasta - preferably rotini, cooked al dente
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring veal chops to room temperature. Season with freshly ground coarse black pepper on both sides, pressing pepper into chops.
2. Melt butter and olive oil in a large, deep-sided frying pan. Brown chops on medium-high heat until well browned, about 5 minutes per side. Remove and cover with plastic wrap to keep warm.
3. Add chopped shallots to the frying pan and sauté, stirring occasionally, for about 3 minutes.
4. Add garlic and continue sautéing, stirring constantly, another minute or so.
5. Add sliced mushrooms and sauté for 1 to 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
6. Reduce heat to medium and add sun-dried tomatoes, chopped tomatoes, anchovy paste, Tabasco, basil, red wine, and reserved tomato juice. Cook uncovered for approximately 15 minutes. If the sauce gets too thick, thin with remaining tomato juice, keeping in mind that the pasta will thicken the sauce slightly.
7. Taste and correct seasoning, adding more basil if desired along with salt and black pepper.
8. Add veal chops to the sauce mixture, along with any meat juices that have accumulated on the plate, and cook uncovered for another 10 minutes until the chops are heated through. Then flip the chops and cook another 5 minutes uncovered before removing them to a plate and covering with plastic wrap to keep warm.
9. Add cooked pasta to the sauce and heat on medium-low for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the pasta is warm. Taste and correct seasoning.
10. Serve pasta on a large platter with veal chops and sprinkle freshly grated imported Romano cheese on top of the pasta.
11. Serves 4.
12. Serve with Caesar Salad or Spinach Salad and garlic bread. Something lemony would be great for dessert, like Lemon Ice or Lemon Cheesecake.
Recipe by Michelle Bass, derived from Grilled Veal Chops with Double Tomato Relish printed in Bon Appetit, June, 1991.
